pattern: Delete every value that appears more than once in a sorted list, keeping only the values that occur exactly once.
hints: Unlike the simpler variant, a value that repeats is removed entirely — not even one copy survives — so the head itself may need deleting.
A dummy node before the head gives you a stable predecessor so you can drop a run that starts at the very first node.
Keep a pointer to the last confirmed-unique node; when you see that the next node begins a duplicate run, skip the whole run and link the predecessor past it.

edge_inputs.description: duplicate run in the middle is fully removed
duplicate run at the head is removed entirely
every value duplicated leaves an empty list

stress.description: long sorted list with many duplicate runs
stress.note: small value range forces frequent multi-occurrence values

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def deleteDuplicates(self, head):
        vals = []
        node = head
        while node:
            vals.append(node.val)
            node = node.next
        from collections import Counter
        counts = Counter(vals)
        kept = [v for v in vals if counts[v] == 1]
        out = None
        for v in reversed(kept):
            out = ListNode(v, out)
        return out
